name: Emergence of Leaders II: Bhagat Singh, Bharathiar, V.O.Chidambaranar, Kamarajar, Periyar, Rajaji, Azad, Tagore

2025_q1 page 106 Q171 (Periyar fought patriarchal chauvinism, with answer key)

Wikipedia/Britannica - Kamaraj Plan 1963, Self-Respect Movement founding, Bharathiar's other works (Panchali Sabatham etc.), Bhagat Singh's Legislative Assembly bombing, Azad's Al-Hilal journal, Tagore's Bangladesh anthem

Wikipedia (Naujawan Bharat Sabha, C. Rajagopalachari, Rajmohan Gandhi), dtnext.in/tnpscthervupettagam.com - Bharathiar's 'Bharati' title origin, Rajaji's full political career and unique Governor-General distinction, Kula Kalvi Thittam controversy, Kamarajar's school-expansion response, Rajmohan Gandhi's dual family link

researchNotes: Second of two 'Emergence of Leaders' topics, completing the syllabus's named-leader list. 3 real hits cluster heavily around Periyar and Rajaji (both confirmed via genuine match-the-following questions); the remaining leaders rest on standard, well-established biographical facts. Expanded 2026-07 pass: rounded out each leader with one more high-yield fact — Bharathiar's other major works, the precise founding of the Self-Respect Movement (a genuine exam trap: founded by S. Ramanathan in 1925, who then invited Periyar to lead it — not founded BY Periyar himself), the 1963 Kamaraj Plan, V.O.C.'s 'Kappalottiya Tamizhan' title, Bhagat Singh's Legislative Assembly bombing (distinct from the Saunders assassination), Azad's 1912 Al-Hilal journal, and Tagore's rare distinction of having written two countries' national anthems. Round-2 pass (2026-07, second expansion): added the Naujawan Bharat Sabha (Bhagat Singh's public-facing organization, distinct from the HRA/HSRA), the precise origin of Bharathiar's 'Bharati' title, Rajaji's full career arc culminating in his unique Governor-General distinction, the Kula Kalvi Thittam controversy and Kamarajar's direct policy response to it (linking two leaders' facts together), and the Rajmohan Gandhi family-connection trivia (grandson of both Gandhi and Rajaji). Roughly doubled mock questions (16→22).

reading.intro: Second 'Emergence of Leaders' topic, covering more Tamil-Nadu-rooted figures plus Bhagat Singh, Azad, and Tagore. Real exam evidence clusters around Periyar and Rajaji specifically via match-the-following formats — a useful signal that TNPSC tests these two especially closely.

qa.question: Real exam: Which work is associated with Bharathiar (matched correctly in a real exam against several other Tamil literary figures)?
qa.answer: Kannan Pattu — one of Bharathiar's most famous poem collections, portraying Lord Krishna in many human relationships (as friend, lover, child, master) as a vehicle for his nationalist and devotional themes.

qa.question: Real exam: Which journal is associated with Thanthai Periyar, and what social struggle is he specifically noted for in another real exam question?
qa.answer: Kudiyarasu (a journal he edited, used to spread his rationalist and Self-Respect Movement ideas). He is separately confirmed in a real exam as the leader who 'fought against patriarchal chauvinism'.

qa.question: Real exam: Which political party did C. Rajagopalachari (Rajaji) found?
qa.answer: The Swatantra Party (a free-market, anti-socialist party he founded after leaving Congress).

qa.question: What company did V.O. Chidambaranar (V.O.C.) found, and why was it significant?
qa.answer: The Swadeshi Steam Navigation Company — founded to challenge British shipping dominance along the Indian coast, a direct economic act of resistance (earning him the title 'Kappalottiya Tamizhan', 'the Tamil helmsman').

qa.question: What is Kamarajar best known for, both politically and in terms of policy?
qa.answer: Known as the 'Kingmaker' in Indian politics (instrumental in selecting Prime Ministers after Nehru). As Tamil Nadu Chief Minister, he strongly promoted education and launched the Midday Meal Scheme to improve school enrollment and child nutrition.

qa.question: What was Maulana Abul Kalam Azad's key post-independence role, and his role in the freedom struggle?
qa.answer: He was independent India's first Education Minister. During the freedom struggle, he was a senior Congress leader and played a key role in the Quit India Movement (1942).

qa.question: What is Rabindranath Tagore best known for internationally and in India?
qa.answer: The first non-European to win the Nobel Prize in Literature (1913, for Gitanjali). He also composed 'Jana Gana Mana', India's national anthem.

qa.question: What is Bhagat Singh best known for, and how did his execution come about?
qa.answer: A member of the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (HSRA, formed 1928 with Chandrashekhar Azad, Sukhdev, and Rajguru). He and Rajguru assassinated British police officer J.P. Saunders (December 1928) in revenge for the death of Lala Lajpat Rai. Tried in the Lahore Conspiracy Case, Bhagat Singh, Rajguru, and Sukhdev were executed on 23 March 1931.

qa.question: Besides 'Kannan Pattu', what other major works did Bharathiar write?
qa.answer: Panchali Sabatham (a retelling of the Mahabharata's dice-game episode, celebrated as an early Tamil feminist classic on women's liberation), Kuyil Pattu, Paapa Pattu, and Tamil translations of the Bhagavad Gita and Patanjali's Yoga Sutras.

qa.question: When and by whom was the Self-Respect Movement founded, and what was Periyar's exact role?
qa.answer: Founded in 1925 by S. Ramanathan, who then invited E.V. Ramasamy (Periyar) to lead it in Tamil Nadu. Periyar became its most famous face and shaped its rationalist, anti-caste ideology, but he did not found it himself — a common exam trap.
qa.tip: Exam trap: the Self-Respect Movement was FOUNDED by S. Ramanathan, who then invited Periyar to lead it — don't default to 'Periyar founded it' just because he's the name most associated with it.

qa.question: What was the Kamaraj Plan (1963), and what did it achieve?
qa.answer: Proposed by K. Kamarajar in 1963, urging senior Congress leaders — including himself, as Tamil Nadu's Chief Minister — to resign from government posts and dedicate themselves to party organizational work instead. Six Union Ministers and six Chief Ministers followed suit; seen as a moral gesture prioritizing party renewal over personal power.

qa.question: What title is V.O. Chidambaranar popularly known by, and why?
qa.answer: 'Kappalottiya Tamizhan' ('the Tamil helmsman/shipman'), for founding and personally piloting the Swadeshi Steam Navigation Company's ships in defiance of British shipping monopoly along the Indian coast.

qa.question: What other major act, besides the Saunders assassination, is Bhagat Singh remembered for?
qa.answer: Along with Batukeshwar Dutt, he threw a deliberately non-lethal, low-intensity bomb into the Central Legislative Assembly, Delhi (April 1929), to protest the repressive Public Safety Bill — famously 'to make the deaf hear'. Both then surrendered voluntarily rather than fleeing, using the resulting trial as a political platform.

qa.question: What did Maulana Azad found in 1912, well before independence?
qa.answer: The Urdu weekly journal 'Al-Hilal', used to spread nationalist and pan-Islamic ideas and criticize British colonial rule.

qa.question: Besides literature, what else is Rabindranath Tagore especially notable for?
qa.answer: He founded Visva-Bharati University at Santiniketan, was also a painter and composer, and — beyond composing India's national anthem — his work also became the basis for Bangladesh's national anthem, 'Amar Shonar Bangla', making him one of the very few people to have authored two countries' national anthems.
qa.tip: Memory hook: Tagore = TWO national anthems (India's 'Jana Gana Mana' AND Bangladesh's 'Amar Shonar Bangla') — a distinctive, easy-to-recall exam fact.

qa.question: Round-2 pass — What was the Naujawan Bharat Sabha, and what was Bhagat Singh's role in it?
qa.answer: Founded by Bhagat Singh in March 1926 as a more public-facing youth organization spreading revolutionary and Marxist ideas among workers and peasants — effectively the open/legal face of the more secretive Hindustan Republican Association. Kedarnath Sehgal was its first president, with Bhagat Singh serving as general secretary.

qa.question: At what age was Subramania Bharati (Bharathiar) conferred the title 'Bharati', and what does it mean?
qa.answer: At age 11, he was conferred the title 'Bharati' ('one blessed by Saraswati, the goddess of learning') by the court of the Raja of Ettayapuram — the origin of the pen name 'Bharathiar'/'Mahakavi Bharati' by which he's known ever since.

qa.question: What government positions did C. Rajagopalachari (Rajaji) hold across his long career, and what unique distinction does he hold?
qa.answer: Premier of the Madras Presidency (1937-39, after the first provincial elections under the 1935 Act), independent India's Governor of West Bengal (1947-48), and later Chief Minister of Madras State (1952-54) — but his most unique distinction is being India's ONLY Indian-born Governor-General (1948-50), the last person to hold that post before it was abolished when India became a republic in 1950.

qa.question: What was the 'Kula Kalvi Thittam' (1953), and what happened to it?
qa.answer: A controversial Modified Scheme of Elementary Education introduced by Rajaji's Madras State government in 1953, splitting the school day into two sessions — one for regular teaching, one where students would learn their parents' traditional occupations. Criticized as reinforcing hereditary caste-based occupations ('hereditary education policy'), it was scrapped once Kamarajar became Chief Minister.

qa.question: How did Kamarajar's education policy as Chief Minister differ from and respond to Rajaji's Kula Kalvi Thittam?
qa.answer: Kamarajar scrapped the Kula Kalvi Thittam scheme and instead undertook large-scale expansion of schooling — opening around 12,000 new schools — alongside his Midday Meal Scheme, prioritizing universal access to standard education over occupation-based tracking.

qa.question: What is Rajmohan Gandhi's connection to two of the leaders covered in this pair of topics?
qa.answer: Rajmohan Gandhi is the grandson of BOTH Mahatma Gandhi (his paternal grandfather) and C. Rajagopalachari/Rajaji (his maternal grandfather) — a notable family link between two separately covered leaders. He won the Sahitya Akademi Award for his biography 'Rajaji: A Life'.

questions.prompt: Which famous work is associated with Bharathiar?
Thanthai Periyar is associated with which journal?
Periyar is specifically noted, per a real exam question, for fighting against:
C. Rajagopalachari (Rajaji) founded which political party?
V.O. Chidambaranar founded which shipping company to challenge British shipping dominance?
Kamarajar is best known in Indian politics as the:
Maulana Abul Kalam Azad served as independent India's first:
Rabindranath Tagore won the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1913 for which work?
Bhagat Singh and Rajguru assassinated which British officer in December 1928?
questions.explanation: J.P. Saunders (mistakenly targeted instead of the intended James Scott).

questions.prompt: Which of Bharathiar's works retells the Mahabharata's dice-game episode and is regarded as an early Tamil feminist classic?
The Self-Respect Movement was founded in 1925 by:
questions.explanation: S. Ramanathan founded it in 1925 and then invited Periyar to lead it in Tamil Nadu.
questions.trick: Classic trap: don't assume the person most associated with a movement is automatically its founder.

questions.prompt: The 1963 Kamaraj Plan asked senior Congress leaders to:
options: Resign from government posts for party organizational work
questions.explanation: Resign from government posts to strengthen party organizational work.

questions.prompt: V.O. Chidambaranar is popularly known by which title?
Bhagat Singh and Batukeshwar Dutt threw a (deliberately non-lethal) bomb in which building in April 1929?
questions.explanation: Central Legislative Assembly, Delhi — to protest the Public Safety Bill.

questions.prompt: Maulana Azad founded which Urdu weekly journal in 1912?
Besides India's national anthem, Rabindranath Tagore's work also became the national anthem of which other country?
questions.trick: Tagore = 2 national anthems (India + Bangladesh) — a distinctive, memorable exam fact.

questions.prompt: The Naujawan Bharat Sabha, founded by Bhagat Singh in March 1926, served as:
options: The more public-facing youth wing spreading revolutionary ideas, linked to the HRA
questions.explanation: A public-facing youth organization for revolutionary/Marxist ideas, effectively an open face of the more secretive HRA.

questions.prompt: At age 11, Subramania Bharati was conferred the title 'Bharati', meaning:
options: One blessed by Saraswati, goddess of learning
questions.explanation: One blessed by Saraswati, goddess of learning — the origin of his pen name.

questions.prompt: C. Rajagopalachari (Rajaji) holds which unique distinction among Indian leaders?
questions.explanation: India's only Indian-born Governor-General (1948-50), the post's last holder before it was abolished in 1950.

questions.prompt: Rajaji's 1953 'Kula Kalvi Thittam' scheme was criticized for:
options: Reinforcing hereditary, caste-based occupational tracking in schools
questions.explanation: It split the school day so students would also learn their parents' traditional occupations, seen as reinforcing hereditary caste roles.

questions.prompt: As Chief Minister, Kamarajar responded to Rajaji's Kula Kalvi Thittam by:
options: Expanding and strengthening the same scheme
Scrapping it and opening around 12,000 new schools
questions.explanation: He scrapped the scheme and expanded schooling access broadly.

questions.prompt: Rajmohan Gandhi, author of a Sahitya Akademi Award-winning biography of Rajaji, is related to which TWO leaders covered across these two 'Emergence of Leaders' topics?
options: Mahatma Gandhi (paternal grandfather) and Rajaji (maternal grandfather)
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi and C. Rajagopalachari (Rajaji) were his paternal and maternal grandfathers respectively.
